# Flask-APScheduler

Flask-APScheduler is a Flask extension which adds support for the APScheduler.

{{ Version }} {{ Tests }} {{ Coverage }} {{ Docs }}

## Features

- Loads scheduler configuration from Flask configuration.
- Loads job definitions from Flask configuration.
- Allows to specify the hostname which the scheduler will run on.
- Provides a REST API to manage the scheduled jobs.
- Provides authentication for the REST API.
- Integrates with [Flask Blueprints](https://github.com/viniciuschiele/flask-apscheduler/tree/master/examples/application_factory)

## Installation

You can install Flask-APScheduler via Python Package Index ([PyPI])

```python
pip install Flask-APScheduler
```

## Documentation

[See Flask APSchedulers Documentation.](https://viniciuschiele.github.io/flask-apscheduler/)

## Feedback

Please use the [Issues] for feature requests and troubleshooting usage.

[examples]: https://github.com/viniciuschiele/flask-apscheduler/tree/master/examples
[issues]: https://github.com/viniciuschiele/flask-apscheduler/issues
[pypi]: https://pypi.python.org/pypi/Flask-APScheduler

functions.name: flask_apscheduler.api.pause_scheduler
documentation:
Pauses job processing in the scheduler.

This will prevent the scheduler from waking up to do job processing until {meth}`resume`
is called. It will not however stop any already running job processing.

methods.name: flask_apscheduler.scheduler.APScheduler.start
documentation:
Start the scheduler.
:param bool paused: if True, don't start job processing until resume is called.

methods.name: flask_apscheduler.scheduler.APScheduler.shutdown
documentation:
Shut down the scheduler. Does not interrupt any currently running jobs.

```
param bool wait

True to wait until all currently executing jobs have finished

raises SchedulerNotRunningError

if the scheduler has not been started yet
```

methods.name: flask_apscheduler.scheduler.APScheduler.pause
documentation:
Pause job processing in the scheduler.

This will prevent the scheduler from waking up to do job processing until {meth}`resume`
is called. It will not however stop any already running job processing.

methods.name: flask_apscheduler.scheduler.APScheduler.resume
documentation: Resume job processing in the scheduler.

methods.name: flask_apscheduler.scheduler.APScheduler.add_listener
documentation:
Add a listener for scheduler events.

When a matching event occurs, `callback` is executed with the event object as its
sole argument. If the `mask` parameter is not provided, the callback will receive events
of all types.

For further info: <https://apscheduler.readthedocs.io/en/latest/userguide.html#scheduler-events>

```
param callback

any callable that takes one argument

param int mask

bitmask that indicates which events should be listened to
```

methods.name: flask_apscheduler.scheduler.APScheduler.remove_listener
documentation: Remove a previously added event listener.

methods.name: flask_apscheduler.scheduler.APScheduler.add_job
documentation:
Add the given job to the job list and wakes up the scheduler if it's already running.

```
param str id

explicit identifier for the job (for modifying it later)

param func

callable (or a textual reference to one) to run at the given time
```

methods.name: flask_apscheduler.scheduler.APScheduler.remove_job
documentation:
Remove a job, preventing it from being run any more.

:param str id: the identifier of the job
:param str jobstore: alias of the job store that contains the job

methods.name: flask_apscheduler.scheduler.APScheduler.remove_all_jobs
documentation:
Remove all jobs from the specified job store, or all job stores if none is given.

:param str|unicode jobstore: alias of the job store

methods.name: flask_apscheduler.scheduler.APScheduler.get_job
documentation:
Return the Job that matches the given `id`.

```
param str id

the identifier of the job

param str jobstore

alias of the job store that most likely contains the job

return

the Job by the given ID, or None if it wasn't found

rtype

Job
```

methods.name: flask_apscheduler.scheduler.APScheduler.get_jobs
documentation:
Return a list of pending jobs (if the scheduler hasn't been started yet) and scheduled jobs, either from a
specific job store or from all of them.

```
param str jobstore

alias of the job store

rtype

list[Job]
```

methods.name: flask_apscheduler.scheduler.APScheduler.modify_job
documentation:
Modify the properties of a single job. Modifications are passed to this method as extra keyword arguments.

:param str id: the identifier of the job
:param str jobstore: alias of the job store that contains the job

methods.name: flask_apscheduler.scheduler.APScheduler.pause_job
documentation:
Pause the given job until it is explicitly resumed.

:param str id: the identifier of the job
:param str jobstore: alias of the job store that contains the job

methods.name: flask_apscheduler.scheduler.APScheduler.resume_job
documentation:
Resume the schedule of the given job, or removes the job if its schedule is finished.

:param str id: the identifier of the job
:param str jobstore: alias of the job store that contains the job

methods.name: flask_apscheduler.scheduler.APScheduler.run_job
documentation:
Run the given job without scheduling it.
\:param id: the identifier of the job.
\:param str jobstore: alias of the job store that contains the job
\:return:
